name: Columbus (United States)

colors.symbolism: from the flag of Italy, honouring Christopher Columbus
from the flag of Italy; bears the central seal

description: Three vertical stripes — green at the hoist, white in the centre, and red at the fly — charged at the centre with the seal of the City of Columbus.
history: Designed by sign-painter and artist John C. Dornenburg and adopted by the Columbus City Council on 23 February 1929. The Italian-flag colours honour the city's namesake, the Italian-born explorer Christopher Columbus.

features.symbolism: The green-white-red tricolour recalls the flag of Italy, in honour of Christopher Columbus, the city's namesake.

features.role: Seal of the City of Columbus
features.symbolism: The seal depicts a stylised representation of Christopher Columbus's ships and other elements relating to the city's heritage; encircled by the city's name and founding date.
